items.title: Oil prices spike to highest levels in years as Iran war intensifies
items.summary_auto: Oil prices spiked near $120 US per barrel before falling back Monday as the Iran war intensified, threatening production and shipping in the Middle East and pummeling financial markets.

items.title: At least 42 people killed in Kenya floods
items.summary_auto: The death toll from heavy rains and flooding in Kenya's capital, Nairobi, and elsewhere has nearly doubled to 42, the government said ‌in a statement issued late on Sunday.

items.title: How good is Alberta 'tax advantage' when it requires deficit borrowing?
items.summary_auto: Alberta has a revenue problem, a spending problem and 'budget amnesia,' says a former senior UCP advisor and Finance official.

items.title: Canadian military personnel identified on white supremacist dating site
items.summary_auto: Members of the Canadian Armed Forces are among the profiles leaked from a white-only dating website that promoted white supremacist ideology, a CBC News visual investigation found.

items.title: Ontario premier defends health-care record as hospitals say financial 'crisis' is coming
items.summary_auto: Premier Doug Ford is defending his government’s health-care record ahead of his eighth budget, even as the province’s hospitals say they face a billion-dollar structural funding deficit.

items.title: New tuition scam left woman with $37K in fraudulent charges – and no help from bank, police or school
items.summary_auto: A new scam is emerging across Canada – fraudsters who claim to give students a tuition break, but rip them off using stolen credit cards. When a Vancouver woman discovered $37,000 in fraudulent charges on her accounts, neither the university involved, police, nor TD Bank said they could help.

items.title: How are MPs passing so many bills without voting?
items.summary_auto: MPs don't officially have to vote in order to pass bills in the House of Commons, and it appears to be happening more frequently than usual this session of Parliament.

items.title: Tens of thousands hit Mexico's streets to mark International Women's Day
items.summary_auto: Tens of thousands of women marched through Mexico City and across the country Sunday to mark International Women’s Day in what has become the biggest political protest event in a country where human rights advocates say gender-based violence often goes ignored or unpunished.

items.title: Pulsing wristbands, military jet and foreign dignitaries add electricity to Arctic Winter Games in Whitehorse
Matt Dunstone captures 1st Brier title, defeating 4-time champion Kevin Koe
items.summary_auto: Matt Dunstone and his Manitoba curling team won the Canadian men's championship on Sunday evening, defeating Alberta's Kevin Koe 6-3 in St. John's.

items.title: What we know about Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei
items.summary_auto: Iran on Monday named Mojtaba Khamenei to succeed his father, Ali Khamenei, as supreme leader, signalling that hardliners remain firmly in charge in Tehran more than a week ‌into its conflict with the United States and Israel.

items.title: Damaging winds leave some Albertans without power
items.summary_auto: Environment Canada is warning residents in parts of Alberta to secure loose objects and expect damage as strong winds whip through the region, while leaving thousands of residents in the dark.

items.title: Bystanders help save two youth who fell through ice, Hamilton police say
items.summary_auto: An officer with Hamilton police's marine unit says no ice is safe after bystanders helped rescue two youth who fell through the ice near the Pier 7 boardwalk.

items.title: 2 arrested after explosive device thrown at protest near Mayor Mamdani's house: NYPD
items.summary_auto: New York ​City police said on Sunday a device that was ignited and thrown during protests outside Mayor Zohran Mamdani's home over the weekend was an explosive that could have caused serious injury or death.

items.title: Ontario police rescue 23 people stranded on ice shelf that separated from shore in Georgian Bay
items.summary_auto: Provincial police say 23 people were rescued after a piece of ice they were standing on separated from shore in Georgian Bay.

items.title: Ontario police rescue 23 people stranded on ice shelf that separated from shore
items.summary_auto: Provincial police say 23 people were rescued after a piece of ice they were standing on separated from shore in Georgian Bay.

items.title: As war takes its toll in Iran, some still hope for regime change
items.summary_auto: Iran's ambassador to the United Nations said 1,332 Iranian civilians have been killed in the conflict so far. With the war now in its second week, some opponents of Iran's governing regime are conflicted by the U.S.-Israeli attacks on their country.

items.title: Sask. trained Kurt Oatway takes bronze in Paralympic skiing
items.summary_auto: Kurt Oatway, the 2018 Paralympic gold medal winner who trained in Saskatchewan has just medalled again, taking bronze in men's downhill sit-skiing.

items.title: Canadian Para alpine star Mollie Jepsen to miss rest of Milano-Cortina Games with knee injury
items.summary_auto: Mollie Jepsen's Paralympics have come to an early end due to a knee injury. The Canadian Paralympic Committee and Alpine Canada announced that the West Vancouver, B.C., native will not compete in the four events remaining on her schedule.

items.title: Anxiety in some Alberta mountain towns as wildfire season begins
items.summary_auto: Despite spring snow still dusting parts of Alberta, it's now officially wildfire season, and with it comes once again the gnawing worry about which community will next be touched by nature's fickle finger of destruction.

items.title: Roaring crowd of 8,992 sets Para hockey attendance record at U.S.-Italy game in Milan
items.summary_auto: The opening Para ice hockey match at the Milano-Cortina Winter Games set an attendance record for the sport at a major tournament.

items.title: Former rapper and Gen Z's candidate poised to win Nepali election by landslide
items.summary_auto: In a resounding victory that is guaranteed to reshape Nepal's politics, rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah appeared headed for a landslide win in the South Asian country's first polls since youth-led protests toppled the previous government last fall.
